cards.title: Turing Test
cards.body: The Turing Test measures a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human.

cards.title: Grace Hopper
cards.body: Grace Hopper developed the first compiler, revolutionizing programming with her work on the Harvard Mark I computer.

cards.title: World Wide Web
cards.body: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee, enabling users to access and share information via web browsers and hyperlinks.

cards.title: Cybersecurity
cards.body: The first computer virus, Creeper, was discovered in 1971 and was an experimental self-replicating virus.

cards.title: Hash Function
cards.body: A hash function is a one-way mathematical function that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size output, known as a hash value.

cards.title: Cloud Computing
cards.body: Cloud computing allows users to store and process data remotely, using a network of servers accessed over the internet.

cards.title: Programming Paradigm
cards.body: Object-oriented programming is a paradigm that organizes software design around data, or objects, and their interactions.

cards.title: Data Encryption
cards.body: Public-key cryptography uses a pair of keys, one public and one private, to securely encrypt and decrypt data.

cards.title: Human-Computer Interaction
cards.body: The field of human-computer interaction focuses on designing interfaces that are intuitive, user-friendly, and accessible to users.

cards.title: Internet of Things
cards.body: The Internet of Things refers to the network of physical devices, vehicles, and other items embedded with sensors and connected to the internet.

quizzes.question: What is the primary goal of the Turing Test?
options: To exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to a human
quizzes.explanation: The Turing Test is designed to assess a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human.

quizzes.question: Who invented the World Wide Web?
quizzes.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee is credited with inventing the World Wide Web, enabling users to access and share information via web browsers and hyperlinks.

quizzes.question: What is the primary function of a hash function?
quizzes.explanation: A hash function is a one-way mathematical function that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size output, known as a hash value.

quizzes.question: What programming paradigm organizes software design around data, or objects, and their interactions?
quizzes.explanation: Object-oriented programming is a paradigm that organizes software design around data, or objects, and their interactions.

quizzes.question: What field of study focuses on designing interfaces that are intuitive, user-friendly, and accessible to users?
quizzes.explanation: The field of human-computer interaction focuses on designing interfaces that are intuitive, user-friendly, and accessible to users.

guesses.prompt: In what year was the first computer virus discovered?
guesses.explanation: The first computer virus, Creeper, was discovered in 1971 and was an experimental self-replicating virus.

guesses.prompt: What percentage of devices are estimated to be connected to the Internet of Things?
guesses.explanation: The Internet of Things refers to the network of physical devices, vehicles, and other items embedded with sensors and connected to the internet, with an estimated 10% of devices already connected.
